words.def: to have the skill, intelligence, opportunity, etc. needed to do something
words.example: You must be able to speak French for this job.

words.def: at or to a higher place
words.example: Put it on the shelf above.

words.def: used to emphasize that something is completely true
words.example: You're absolutely right.

words.def: the opportunity or right to use something or to see somebody/something
words.example: High-speed internet access has become a necessity.

words.def: a thing that somebody has done successfully, especially using their own effort and skill
words.example: the greatest scientific achievement of the decade

words.def: a particular thing that somebody does
words.example: You have committed a serious criminal act.

words.def: always busy doing things, especially physical activities
words.example: Staying physically active in later years can also keep you feeling younger.

words.def: a woman who performs on the stage, on television or in films, especially as a profession
words.example: The Oscar-winning actress has taken a break from acting.

words.def: a notice, picture or film telling people about a product, job or service
words.example: The TV ads were first run last year.

words.def: a thing that is added to something else
words.example: All of these technologies are fairly recent additions.

words.def: to respect somebody for what they have done or to respect their qualities
words.example: admire somebody/something, I really admire your enthusiasm.

words.def: to agree, often unwillingly, that something is true
words.example: It was a stupid thing to do, I admit.

words.def: having the most modern and recently developed ideas, methods, etc.
words.example: Scientists are working on highly advanced technology to replace fossil fuels.

words.def: to tell the public about a product or a service in order to encourage people to buy or to use it
words.example: If you want to attract customers you need to advertise.

words.def: to tell somebody what you think they should do in a particular situation
words.example: advise against doing something, I would strongly advise against going out on your own.

words.def: to produce a change in somebody/something
words.example: How will these changes affect us?

words.def: to have enough money to be able to buy or do something
words.example: afford something, Can we afford a new car?

words.def: the number of years that a person has lived or a thing has existed
words.example: You're the same age as my brother.

words.def: of the age of
words.example: They have two children aged six and nine.

words.def: a person whose job is to act for, or manage the affairs of, other people in business, politics, etc.
words.example: Our agent in New York deals with all US sales.

words.def: an arrangement, a promise or a contract made with somebody
words.example: an international peace agreement

words.def: further forward in space or time; in front
words.example: I'll run ahead and warn them.

words.def: the purpose of doing something; what somebody is trying to achieve
words.example: the stated aims of the study

words.def: a company that provides regular flights to take passengers and goods to different places
words.example: an airline pilot/passenger

words.def: a book in which you keep photographs, stamps, etc.
words.example: a photo album

words.def: drinks such as beer, wine, etc. that can make people drunk
words.example: He never drinks alcohol.

words.def: connected with or containing alcohol
words.example: alcoholic drinks/beverages

words.def: that can be used instead of something else
words.example: The road was closed so we had to find an alternative route.

words.def: very surprised
words.example: an amazed look

words.def: very surprising, especially in a way that you like or admire
words.example: an amazing feat/story/experience

words.def: something that you want to do or achieve very much
words.example: She had fulfilled her lifelong ambition.

words.def: determined to be successful, rich, powerful, etc.
words.example: a fiercely ambitious young manager

words.def: to examine the nature or structure of something, especially by separating it into its parts, in order to understand or explain it
words.example: analyse something, The job involves collecting and analysing data.

words.def: the detailed study or examination of something in order to understand more about it; the result of the study
words.example: statistical/data analysis

words.def: to tell people something officially, especially about a decision, plans, etc.
words.example: announce something, He officially announced his intention to resign at today's press conference.

words.def: a spoken or written statement that informs people about something
words.example: to make an announcement

words.def: to make somebody slightly angry
words.example: annoy somebody, His constant joking was beginning to annoy her.

words.def: slightly angry
words.example: annoyed with somebody at/about something, He was beginning to get very annoyed with me about my carelessness.

words.def: making somebody feel slightly angry
words.example: This interruption is very annoying.

words.def: separated by a distance, of space or time
words.example: The two houses stood 500 metres apart.

words.def: to say that you are sorry for doing something wrong or causing a problem
words.example: Why should I apologize?

words.def: a formal (often written) request for something, such as a job, permission to do something or a place at a college or university
words.example: a planning/patent/visa application

words.def: a formal arrangement to meet or visit somebody at a particular time, especially for a reason connected with their work
words.example: I've got a dental appointment at 3 o'clock.

words.def: to recognize the good qualities of somebody/something
words.example: Over the years he came to appreciate the beauty and tranquillity of the river.

words.def: used to show that something is almost, but not completely, accurate or correct
words.example: The journey took approximately seven hours.

words.def: a person whose job is designing buildings
words.example: The house was designed by architect Louis Kahn.

words.def: a large organized group of soldiers who are trained to fight on land
words.example: The two opposing armies faced each other across the battlefield.

words.def: to plan or organize something in advance
words.example: arrange something, The party was arranged quickly.

words.def: a plan or preparation that you make so that something can happen
words.example: Travel and accommodation arrangements have been finalized.

words.def: the act of arresting somebody
words.example: The police made several arrests.

words.def: an act of coming or being brought to a place
words.example: We apologize for the late arrival of the train.

words.def: a person who creates works of art, especially paintings or drawings
words.example: an exhibition of work by contemporary British artists

words.def: a task or piece of work that somebody is given to do, usually as part of their job or studies
words.example: Students are required to complete all homework assignments.

words.def: to help somebody to do something
words.example: Anyone willing to assist can contact this number.

words.def: the mixture of gases that surrounds the earth
words.example: Wind power doesn't release carbon dioxide into the atmosphere.

words.def: to fasten or join one thing to another
words.example: attach something, I attach a copy of my notes for your information.

words.def: to be present at an event
words.example: We'd like as many people as possible to attend.

words.def: the way that you think and feel about somebody/something; the way that you behave towards somebody/something that shows how you think and feel
words.example: attitude towards somebody/something, These societies have to change their attitudes towards women.

words.def: if you are attracted by something, it interests you and makes you want it; if you are attracted by somebody, you like or admire them
words.example: be attracted by somebody/something, I had always been attracted by the idea of working abroad.

words.def: an interesting or lively place to go or thing to do
words.example: Buckingham Palace is a major tourist attraction.

words.def: the power to give orders to people
words.example: in a position of authority

words.def: that you can get, buy or find
words.example: available resources/data

words.def: calculated by adding several amounts together, finding a total, and dividing the total by the number of amounts
words.example: The average age of participants was 52 years.

words.def: a prize such as money, etc. for something that somebody has done
words.example: He was nominated for the best actor award.

words.def: knowing or realizing something
words.example: As you're aware, this is not a new problem.

words.def: to cook food in an oven without extra fat or liquid; to be cooked in this way
words.example: bake (something), to bake bread/biscuits/cookies

words.def: a situation in which different things exist in equal, correct or good amounts
words.example: This newspaper maintains a good balance in its presentation of different opinions.
